edges.description: Peroxisomal transport failure drives toxic substrate accumulation in the nervous system and adrenal tissue.
edges.target: VLCFA accumulation in brain and spinal cord

edges.description: VLCFA excess promotes redox imbalance and oxidative injury.
edges.source: VLCFA accumulation in brain and spinal cord

edges.description: VLCFA accumulation is required for X-ALD phenotypes and contributes to the spinal-cord axonopathy branch through oxidative stress, energy shortage, and axon-glial interactions.
intermediate_mechanisms: Oxidative stress and energy shortage in axons.
edges.source: VLCFA accumulation in brain and spinal cord

edges.description: VLCFA accumulation also injures the adrenal cortex, predisposing to adrenal insufficiency.
edges.source: VLCFA accumulation in brain and spinal cord

edges.description: Redox imbalance contributes to astrocytic metabolic failure and inflammatory activation.
Axonal oxidative stress and energy shortage are implicated in the dying-back axonopathy that underlies the AMN branch of X-ALD.
Astrocyte dysfunction contributes to BBB injury and vascular leakage.
Barrier injury promotes innate immune cell recruitment and activation.
Innate immune activation propagates active white-matter destruction.
The active cerebral inflammatory demyelinating lesion is represented clinically and radiographically as cerebral white-matter disease.
Progressive cerebral demyelination produces loss of cognition and executive function.
White matter and frontal-subcortical injury contributes to behavioral and psychiatric change.
Involvement of visual pathways contributes to progressive visual loss.
Spinal-cord tract axonopathy manifests as slowly progressive myelopathy.
The AMN spinal-cord axonopathy branch includes spasticity as a core manifestation.
In some patients the adrenomyeloneuropathy branch extends to peripheral nerve involvement.
The AMN branch includes autonomic lower-tract involvement represented by bladder and bowel dysfunction.
Adrenal cortex injury manifests clinically as primary adrenal insufficiency.
Elevated plasma VLCFA reports impaired ALDP-mediated peroxisomal VLCFA transport.
Elevated VLCFA measures the accumulating substrate pool represented by the VLCFA accumulation node.
edges.source: VLCFA accumulation in brain and spinal cord

edges.description: Chitotriosidase activity reports the monocyte/macrophage activation branch in active cerebral ALD.
Higher pre-transplant plasma and CSF chitotriosidase predict subsequent functional progression in active C-ALD.
CSF matrix metalloproteinase elevation reports BBB extracellular-matrix breakdown in active cerebral ALD.
Higher CSF MMP concentrations correlate with radiographic inflammation and clinical neurologic severity.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: The genetic bases for all different phenotypic variants of X-ALD are mutations in the gene encoding the peroxisomal ATP-binding cassette (ABC) transporter, ABCD1 (formerly adrenoleukodystrophy protein, ALDP).
ABCD1 | HGNC:61 | adrenoleukodystrophy | MONDO:0018544 | XL | Definitive

nodes.description: Pathogenic ABCD1 variants impair ALDP, an ATP-binding cassette transporter in the peroxisomal membrane that imports CoA-activated very long-chain fatty acids for degradation, initiating the biochemical disease cascade.

biological_processes: very long-chain fatty acid metabolic process

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: ABCD1 transports CoA-activated very long-chain fatty acids from the cytosol into the peroxisome for degradation.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy (X-ALD): clinical presentation and guidelines for diagnosis, follow-up and management.
evidence.snippet: peroxisomal membrane protein ALDP which is involved in the transmembrane transport of very long-chain fatty acids

evidence.reference_title: IPSC-Derived Astrocytes to Model Neuroinflammatory and Metabolic Responses in X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy (X-ALD) is an inherited metabolic disorder caused by pathogenic variants in the ABCD1 gene, leading to accumulation of saturated very long chain fatty acids (VLCFA) in body fluids and tissues including brain and spinal cord.

nodes.description: Adrenal VLCFA accumulation contributes to adrenal cortex dysfunction and steroid deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy in a 20-Year-Old Male With an ABCD1 Gene Mutation: First Case From Pakistan.
evidence.snippet: X-ALD can initially present as Addison's disease (primary adrenal insufficiency) as the accumulation of VLCFA most importantly occurs in the adrenal gland.

nodes.description: Primary adrenal failure is a common initial or isolated manifestation of X-ALD.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy in a 20-Year-Old Male With an ABCD1 Gene Mutation: First Case From Pakistan.
evidence.snippet: X-ALD can initially present as Addison's disease (primary adrenal insufficiency) as the accumulation of VLCFA most importantly occurs in the adrenal gland.

nodes.description: ABCD1 mutations commonly manifest as adrenomyeloneuropathy, a slowly progressive dying-back axonopathy affecting ascending and descending spinal cord tracts and sometimes peripheral nerves.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: The default manifestation of mutations in ABCD1 is adrenomyeloneuropathy, a slowly progressive dying-back axonopathy affecting both ascending and descending spinal cord tracts as well as in some cases, a peripheral neuropathy.

nodes.description: Early allogeneic HSCT can halt progression of active cerebral disease in appropriately selected patients.

evidence.reference_title: Chitotriosidase as a biomarker of cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) can halt disease progression in cerebral ALD (C-ALD) if performed early.

nodes.description: ABCD1-mutant astrocytes exhibit metabolic stress and pro-inflammatory signaling changes that help drive cerebral disease.

evidence.reference_title: IPSC-Derived Astrocytes to Model Neuroinflammatory and Metabolic Responses in X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Mitochondrial function analysis by Seahorse extracellular flux identified increased oxygen consumption and extracellular acidification rates in cALD astrocytes, yet the ATP levels were decreased. Molecular signaling identified increased phosphorylation of STAT3 in cALD astrocytes, and higher proinflammatory cytokine and Toll like receptor (TLR) expression.

nodes.description: Cerebral disease may be accompanied by psychiatric and behavioral disturbance.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy in a 20-Year-Old Male With an ABCD1 Gene Mutation: First Case From Pakistan.
evidence.snippet: psychiatric problems included primarily depressive disorder and mild psychotic behavior.

nodes.description: Bladder and bowel dysfunction are AMN manifestations in the X-ALD spectrum; the ontology mapping uses the closest bladder abnormality term for the lower-tract component of this combined clinical feature.

evidence.reference_title: X-Linked Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: AMN is characterized by leg weakness, spasticity, clumsy gait, pain, and bladder and bowel dysfunction

nodes.description: Matrix metalloproteinase-mediated BBB injury is a distinct early step in cerebral ALD.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebrospinal fluid matrix metalloproteinases are elevated in cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy and correlate with MRI severity and neurologic dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: Neuroimaging in cALD shows inflammatory changes and indicates blood-brain-barrier (BBB) disruption.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebrospinal fluid matrix metalloproteinases are elevated in cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy and correlate with MRI severity and neurologic dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: MMPs were found to be elevated in the CSF of boys with cALD and may mechanistically contribute to the breakdown of the blood-brain-barrier.

evidence.reference_title: Cerebrospinal fluid matrix metalloproteinases are elevated in cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy and correlate with MRI severity and neurologic dysfunction.
evidence.snippet: There were significant elevations of MMP2, MMP9, MMP10, TIMP1, and total protein in the CSF of boys with cALD compared to controls.

readouts.interpretation: CSF matrix metalloproteinase elevation reports BBB extracellular-matrix breakdown in active cerebral ALD.
Higher CSF MMP concentrations correlate with radiographic inflammation and clinical neurologic severity.

evidence.reference_title: Chitotriosidase as a biomarker of cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: We document elevations in chitotriosidase activity in the plasma of patients with C-ALD (n = 38; median activity 1,576 ng/mL/hr) vs. controls (n = 16, median 765 ng/mL/hr, p = 0.0004), and in the CSF of C-ALD patients (n = 38; median activity 4,330 ng/mL/hr) vs. controls (n = 16, median 0 ng/mL/hr, p < 0.0001).

readouts.interpretation: Chitotriosidase activity reports the monocyte/macrophage activation branch in active cerebral ALD.
Higher pre-transplant plasma and CSF chitotriosidase predict subsequent functional progression in active C-ALD.

nodes.description: Cerebral inflammatory disease causes progressive cognitive decline and neurologic dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked Adrenoleukodystrophy in a 20-Year-Old Male With an ABCD1 Gene Mutation: First Case From Pakistan.
evidence.snippet: poor concentration and memory

nodes.description: Gene-modified autologous hematopoietic stem cells are an emerging strategy for cerebral ALD when a matched donor is not available or to improve treatment options.

evidence.reference_title: Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation for Neurological Disorders: A Focus on Inborn Errors of Metabolism.
evidence.snippet: hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy, where autologous hematopoietic stem cells are collected, manipulated ex vivo to overexpress the missing enzyme, and infused back into the patient.

nodes.description: Cerebral ALD is characterized by rapidly progressive inflammatory demyelination.

evidence.reference_title: Chitotriosidase as a biomarker of cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: In 35-40% of children with ALD, an acute inflammatory process occurs in the central nervous system (CNS) leading to demyelination that is rapidly progressive, debilitating and ultimately fatal.

nodes.description: Cerebral X-ALD causes inflammatory demyelinating white-matter disease.

evidence.reference_title: Chitotriosidase as a biomarker of cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: In 35-40% of children with ALD, an acute inflammatory process occurs in the central nervous system (CNS) leading to demyelination that is rapidly progressive, debilitating and ultimately fatal.

nodes.description: Activated innate immune cells drive the inflammatory cerebral lesion in cALD.

evidence.reference_title: Chitotriosidase as a biomarker of cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: As cellular infiltration has been observed in C-ALD, including activation of monocytes and macrophages, we evaluated the activity of chitotriosidase in the plasma and spinal fluid of boys with active C-ALD.

nodes.description: Oxidative stress contributes to cellular injury in X-ALD and amplifies the inflammatory cascade.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Oxidative Stress and Inflammation in X-Link Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: research showed that VLCFA could induce oxidative stress and inflammation, leading to damage.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Cell autonomous processes such as oxidative stress and energy shortage in axons as well as non-cell autonomous processes involving axon-glial interactions seem pertinent to the dying-back axonopathy.

nodes.description: Peripheral neuropathy can accompany the adrenomyeloneuropathy axonopathy branch.

evidence.reference_title: Pathophysiology of X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: a slowly progressive dying-back axonopathy affecting both ascending and descending spinal cord tracts as well as in some cases, a peripheral neuropathy.

nodes.description: Males with X-ALD can develop slowly progressive myelopathy as part of the adrenomyeloneuropathy branch.

evidence.reference_title: X-linked adrenoleukodystrophy (X-ALD): clinical presentation and guidelines for diagnosis, follow-up and management.
evidence.snippet: The clinical spectrum in males with X-ALD ranges from isolated adrenocortical insufficiency and slowly progressive myelopathy to devastating cerebral demyelination.

nodes.description: Spasticity is a core AMN manifestation within the X-ALD spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: X-Linked Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: AMN is characterized by leg weakness, spasticity, clumsy gait, pain, and bladder and bowel dysfunction

nodes.description: Steroid replacement pharmacotherapy is used to promptly treat confirmed primary adrenal insufficiency in X-ALD, with endocrinology-guided screening and management throughout childhood and later life.

evidence.reference_title: X-Linked Adrenoleukodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: confirmed adrenal insufficiency according to published steroid replacement guidelines

nodes.description: Accumulated saturated very long-chain fatty acids are a biochemical hallmark of X-ALD and contribute to tissue injury.

evidence.snippet: AMN and cALD astrocytes exhibited lack of ABCD1 and accumulation of VLCFA, a biochemical hallmark of X-ALD disease.

evidence.snippet: Adrenoleukodystrophy (ALD) is an X-linked peroxisomal disorder characterized by the abnormal beta-oxidation of very long chain fatty acids (VLCFA).

nodes.description: Visual pathway involvement can contribute to progressive vision loss.
